author: @OrionSS7879
text: Everybody has their take but I think there's a difference between Netflix and the other Studio/Streaming services.  The studios were movie/TV show makers that tried to stream them.  They had decades of experience in show creation and were feeling out a new distribution method.  Netflix was a DVD rental company that initiated Streaming as a business model that used a catalog that was 100% licensed.  When the studios pulled back their content Netflix had to scramble for replacement content.  With no experience they threw money at people based on their pitches. And many times in the beginning they made bad calls or didn't get what they thought they were getting.  But they also had some great ones.  Based on the IP destruction from the so called "entertainment experts", I'm willing to give Netflix a chance
